Before collection of specimen, aspirate sufficient amount of liquid heparin 1000 U/mL into a 2. 5 mL syringe.

After collection of specimen, eject all visible bubbles. Remove needle and cap the syringe with plastic cap to prevent air leakage.

Vigorous mixing should be avoided - Arterial puncture and blood from CVP catheters are not suitable. - It is important to draw venous blood from a clean venepuncture into a dry syringe uncontaminated with heparin, intravenous fluids, or other foreign substances - Haemolyzed sample is unsuitable for coagulation studies - Specimen must be received within 4 hours from time of specimen collection

For assessment of secondary fibrinolysis in suspected venous thrombotic embolism (VTE) and monitoring of post-operative thrombotic risk. Elevated levels of D-Dimers, the fibrinolytic degradation products of crosslinked fibrin, are associated with venous thromboembolic disorders such as disseminated intravascular coagulation (DIC), deep venous thrombosis (DVT) and pulmonary embolism (PE). Increased D-Dimer levels have also be used as a predictive indicator in an acute myocardial infarction.
Advance arrangement with core lab needed. Call 7737 during office hours. Very expensive test There should be no visible sign of haemolysis.

Investigation of prolonged APTT. Coagulation inhibitors affecting the APTT may be immediate acting or time dependent. Test plasma containing an immediate acting inhibitor will, when mixed with normal plasma, show little or no correction of the clotting time whereas time dependent inhibitors require a period incubation with normal plasma before they can be detected.

Sample should be collected after a minimum of 48 hr but not longer than 7 days of sexual abstinence. - Condoms must not be used for semen collection - Sample should be sent directly to Core lab (8A Wai Shun Block) within one hour after collection and should be protected from extremes of temperature (i. e. not < 20 or > 40 degrees C) during transport - Date / time of collection should be specified - Prior arrangement with laboratory needed.
Spin immediately and keep serum frozen.
